name: Tharangambadi
description: Also known as Tranquebar, this tranquil coastal town was a Danish colony from 1620 to 1845. It boasts historic forts, old churches, and colonial architecture right on the Coromandel coast.
history: The Danish East India Company established a settlement here in 1620, building Fort Dansborg. The town later came under British control before Indian independence.
rainfall: Moderate to High during NE Monsoon
roadConnectivity: Well connected to Chennai and Pondicherry via ECR.
publicTransport: Buses from major nearby towns like Mayiladuthurai and Karaikal.
travelWarnings: The sea can be rough, swimming is not recommended everywhere
droneRegulations: General rules apply. Exercise caution near heritage structures.
environmentalGuidelines: Keep the beaches clean. Do not litter.
localEtiquette: Respect the tranquility of the town. Dress modestly when visiting churches and temples.
photographyTips: Great for architectural photography and sunrise shots by the fort.

topAttractions.name: New Jerusalem Church
topAttractions.description: Built in 1718, a beautiful mix of Indian and European architecture.

culture: A unique blend of Tamil and Danish colonial culture.

restaurants.name: The Bungalow on the Beach Restaurant

travelTips: Book accommodation in advance if planning to stay at heritage properties.
